authority: NESO methodology made under CUSC post-TMO4+ (CMP434/CMP435); Ofgem-approved
summary: The definitive document for exactly what a project must demonstrate to progress from Gate 1 (indicative) to Gate 2 (firm offer). Sets out the tests applied under both the Readiness limb and the Strategic Alignment limb, including the Energy Density Table, acceptable evidence types, and the governance around reapplications.
scope: Readiness — Land Route (100% acreage secured per the Energy Density Table)
Readiness — Planning Route (validated DCO application or equivalent consent submission)
Strategic Alignment — capacity and technology consistent with the relevant strategic plan
Treatment of storage, DSR, interconnectors, demand

keyParts.name: Energy Density Table
keyParts.detail: Technology-specific MW-per-hectare reference values used to size the land required to qualify on the Land Route.

keyParts.name: Gate 2 assessment window
keyParts.detail: Biannual: projects submit evidence in a defined window, are assessed as a cohort, and receive offers together.

keyParts.name: Strategic Alignment mapping
keyParts.detail: How NESO maps applications to the CP2030 technology/location envelope and the future SSEP/CSNP.
